calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Arbitration Award; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Request For Judicial Notice: Appendix Of Declarations; And Appendix Of Court Reporters Transcript
Off Calendar Jun-01-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Steven Robinson of Law Offices of Joseph L. Alioto and Angela Alioto telephone number 415-434-8700 for all Petitioners/Plaintiffs.
Julia Riechert of Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe LLP telephone number 650-614-7482 for Respondents/Defendants G2 Secure Staff and Shazia Khan.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. PETITIONERS JEFFREY DAMASCO, ZAN WIN NAING, JOSE DE JESUS MORALES, ELSA YAMBAO and EXEQUIEL BAUTISTA Motion To Vacate Arbitration Award is denied. Assuming without deciding that the motion was timely filed and served, petitioners have not shown that there are any grounds to vacate the award. Petitioners' failure to raise their concerns about the asserted inattentiveness of the arbitrator during the hearing precludes them from seeking to vacate the award on that basis. Even if they were not so precluded, petitioners do not point to anything in the transcripts of the hearing or any other objective evidence that the arbitrator was inattentive or, if he was, how his inattentiveness prejudiced them in any way. Petitioners' failure to raise their concerns that the arbitrator assertedly requested "political favors" from Ms. Alioto prior to the issuance of the interim award precludes them from seeking to vacate the award on that basis. Even if they were not so precluded, petitioners do not point to anything in the transcripts of the hearing or any other objective evidence that the arbitrator made the statements attributed to him or, if he did make those statements, how those statements prejudiced them in any way. Assuming without deciding that the standard of review set out in Pearson Dental Supplies, Inc. v. Superior Court (2010) 48 Cal.4th 665, and Richey v. AutNation Inc. (2015) 60 Cal.4th 90 applies to this case rather than the standard of review required by the FAA, there is no evidence that the arbitrator's decision was premised on a clear legal error which denied or curtailed petitioners' right to be heard on their claims. The award shows that the arbitrator understood the decision in Roby v. McKesson Corporation (2009) 47 Cal.4th 686, considered the totality of the evidence, and found that the personnel management actions did not support a finding that respondents communicated a hostile message via biased personnel management actions. Petitioners have not demonstrated that the arbitrator's summary disposition of the harassment claims of petitioners Damasco, Naing and Sandoval was a clear legal error which deprived them of their right to have their claims heard. Petitioners also failed to show how the references in the award to petitioners meeting with their attorneys and filed a lawsuit affected any decision made by the arbitrator. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
